backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the Raj Empire Esports backend, defining data types and logic for user profiles, players, tournaments, registrations, support tickets, OTPs, terms, social links, and admin/user access control using mixed-in storage and authorization modules.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting the refactored 2-step RegisterPage (Details + Done), confirming removal of OTP logic and listing the affected files and components with high confidence.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON-formatted summaries of all frontend source files, describing the purpose and key details of each component, page, hook, context, and config file in the project.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS entry file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes, including colors for background, foreground, card, sidebar, chart, and destructive states.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app, loading Google Fonts (Rajdhani, Orbitron, Saira Condensed) and mounting the app into a root div via main.tsx.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React app component that configures TanStack Router with all routes (home, register, login, tournaments, dashboard, terms, admin login, admin dashboard), wraps everything in QueryClientProvider, AuthProvider, and AdminAuthProvider.
frontend/src/components/AdminProtectedRo: Route guard component that redirects unauthenticated admin users to /admin by checking isAdminAuthenticated from AdminAuthContext.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Site footer displaying brand info, quick navigation links (using useNavigate), and social media icons (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram) fetched via useGetSocialLinks.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky navigation header with logo, desktop/mobile nav links, and conditional auth controls (login/register or player dashboard/logout) based on authentication state.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component that wraps all pages with a Header, Footer, and a flex-column container, rendering child routes via Outlet.
frontend/src/components/PaymentModal.tsx: Modal for tournament registration that lets users upload a payment screenshot, displays a QR code and entry fee, and submits the registration via a mutation with upload progress tracking.
frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.t: Route guard component that redirects unauthenticated users to the login page and renders children only when authenticated.
frontend/src/context/AdminAuthContext.ts: React context providing admin authentication state persisted in localStorage, with login and logout functions and a legacy login alias for backward compatibility.
frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx: React context managing player session state (mobile, displayName, bgmiPlayerId) persisted in localStorage, exposing login and logout helpers.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (anonymous or authenticated) using React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Hook and provider for Internet Identity authentication via DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing identity, login/clear functions, and login status flags.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Collection of React Query hooks for fetching and mutating backend data including tournaments, registrations, players, social links, and terms, all wired through the useActor hook.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet importing Orbitron/Rajdhani/Exo 2 fonts, defining light/dark CSS custom properties, Tailwind layers, scrollbar styles, and brand gradient utilities.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that wraps the App component with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider before mounting to the DOM.
frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.ts: Admin dashboard page with a sidebar navigation supporting tabs for tournaments, registrations, players, terms, support tickets, and social links, using various query/mutation hooks for CRUD operations.
frontend/src/pages/AdminLoginPage.tsx: Two-phase admin login page with hardcoded credentials for each phase; on successful completion of both phases it calls adminLogin() and redirects to the dashboard.
frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx: Player dashboard page with tabs for browsing/filtering tournaments, viewing personal registrations with status badges, and a profile view, including a PaymentModal for tournament registration.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Public landing page featuring a hero section with CTA buttons and a features grid highlighting daily tournaments, mega events, game modes, and platform security.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Player login page that authenticates via Internet Identity, fetches the caller's profile from the backend actor, and redirects to the player dashboard on success.
frontend/src/pages/RegisterPage.tsx: A 3-step player registration page (details → Internet Identity authentication → success) that collects display name, mobile, and BGMI Player ID, then authenticates via Internet Identity before calling registerPlayer and saveProfile mutations.
frontend/src/pages/TermsPage.tsx: Displays the platform's Terms & Conditions by fetching content from the backend (with a hardcoded fallback), parsing numbered sections, and rendering them with alternating colored borders.
frontend/src/pages/TournamentsPage.tsx: Lists all BGMI tournaments with search and status filter controls, showing each tournament as a card with a register button that opens a PaymentModal or redirects unauthenticated users to login.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module providing functions to read URL/hash query parameters, persist them to sessionStorage, and clean sensitive params from the URL hash without page reload.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with custom brand colors (red/orange/dark), Orbitron/Rajdhani/Exo 2 fonts, brand gradient/shadow utilities, and accordion animations.
project_state.json: Project state metadata describing the Empire Esports tournament platform built on Internet Computer/Caffeine, tracking existing features, pending architectural requests (notably replacing OTP with Internet Identity auth), and known issues.
